title: WSL2 and Podman 3.4.0 - Cannot connect to Podman
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

**Description**
Upgrading podman to 3.4.0 on Windows WSL2 causes it to not work anymore


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman per the instructions here https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-windows-wsl2

2. Download the static 3.4.0 version from the releases page and replace existing podman in /usr/bin/ with new version

3. podman info causes error

**Describe the results you received:**
```
 jfmatth@Dad-dell5580  ~  podman --version
podman version 3.4.0
 jfmatth@Dad-dell5580  ~  podman info
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v3.4.0/libpod/_ping": dial unix ///tmp/podman-run--1/podman/podman.sock: connect: no such file or directory
 ✘ jfmatth@Dad-dell5580  ~ 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
When upgrading to 3.4.0 I changed the /usr/bin/podman to a symlink and symlinked to the new 3.4.0 version, but kept the old repository version around (podman.orig)

when running the same commands on 3.3.1, it works fine
```
 ✘ jfmatth@Dad-dell5580  ~  podman.orig --version
podman.orig version 3.3.1
 jfmatth@Dad-dell5580  ~  podman.orig info
WARN[0000] "/" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.22.3
  cgroupControllers: []
  cgroupManager: cgroupfs
...
```

Also, after running the 3.3.1 version, it leaves a running process around, I suspect this is related?
```
 jfmatth@Dad-dell5580  ~  ps -ef
UID        PID  PPID  C STIME TTY          TIME CMD
root         1     0  0 04:51 ?        00:00:00 /init
root         8     1  0 04:51 ?        00:00:00 /init
root         9     8  0 04:51 ?        00:00:00 /init
jfmatth     10     9  0 04:51 pts/0    00:00:00 -zsh
jfmatth    118     9  0 04:52 ?        00:00:00 podman.orig
jfmatth    171     9  0 04:52 ?        00:00:00 /usr/bin/dbus-daemon --syslog --fork --print-pid 4 --print-address 6 --s
jfmatth    182    10  0 04:54 pts/0    00:00:00 ps -ef
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
 jfmatth@Dad-dell5580  ~  podman --version
podman version 3.4.0
 jfmatth@Dad-dell5580  ~ 
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
 jfmatth@Dad-dell5580  ~  podman info --debug
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v3.4.0/libpod/_ping": dial unix ///tmp/podman-run--1/podman/podman.sock: connect: no such file or directory
 ✘ jfmatth@Dad-dell5580  ~ 

```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Windows WSL2

comments.body: The static version is a remote client and not the actual podman.

Well, that explains it :)  Thanks for the quick response.

Any idea when 3.4.0 will be up in that repo?
